repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93897 on the contributor branch so the legacy openai-codex plugin-entry migration preserves data, reports accurate change messages, warns on true conflicts, and includes proof/tests before merge reconsideration.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(doctor): preserve legacy openai-codex plugin entry fields
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
Repair the #93897 migration so dual `plugins.entries.openai` and `plugins.entries.openai-codex` configs are merged without silent data loss.

## Repair scope
- Keep the focused deep-merge behavior from source PR #93897 by @lsr911.
- Return/report accurate change messages for merge vs rewrite paths.
- Surface true key conflicts as warnings instead of silently dropping legacy values.
- Add regression coverage and real behavior proof for the dual-key migration case.

## Credit
Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897 by @lsr911. Issue and review context: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 by @Nas01010101.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`
- targeted provider legacy config migration tests
- real-behavior-proof workflow for #93897
validation_commands: Run the repository real-behavior-proof workflow for #93897 after repair
credit_notes: Preserve source PR credit for @lsr911 as the contributor who introduced the focused deep-merge fix in #93897.
Preserve issue/review attribution for @Nas01010101 from #93891 and the review comments on #93897.
If a replacement PR is opened instead of repairing the branch, the PR body must include #93897 as the source PR and explicitly credit @lsr911.
